gravitr 0.9
  • Reference
  • Examples

Allocate supply and demand using a gravity model with Furness convergence

gravity.Rd

Allocate supply and demand using a gravity model with Furness convergence

Usage

gravity(
  supply,
  demand,
  distance_matrix,
  supply_col = "supply",
  demand_col = "demand",
  distance_power = 2,
  max_iter = 100,
  delta = 0.001,
  min_improvement = 1e-04,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

supply

A data frame containing supply values.

demand

A data frame containing demand values.

distance_matrix

A matrix of distances. Rows must match supply, columns must match demand.

supply_col

Name of the supply column.

demand_col

Name of the demand column.

distance_power

Power applied to distance in the initial gravity formula.

max_iter

Maximum number of iterations.

delta

Convergence threshold.

min_improvement

Minimum relative improvement before stopping.

verbose

If TRUE, prints convergence messages.

Value

A list containing flows, weighted distances, summaries and convergence information.
